spec.goal: Implement XY-162 by preventing self-bootstrap lanes from ending a turn cleanly after coding and verification work unless the lane explicitly records one valid terminal tracker path.
success_criteria: A lane cannot present coding work as complete unless it has explicitly finalized either a PR-backed review handoff or a manual-attention exit for the leased issue.
Saved plan completion cannot be treated as sufficient success when the lane has not recorded a terminal tracker outcome.
Runtime behavior, issue-scoped tool policy, and operator docs all describe the same terminal turn contract.
Regression coverage reproduces the XY-156 shape where implementation and tests finished but commit, push, PR, and tracker handoff remained undone.
constraints: Keep the existing PR-backed In Review handoff model; do not redesign broader delivery or landing automation.
Do not widen scope into unrelated review lifecycle cleanup outside the lane completion contract.
Preserve Maestro ownership of final success writeback and terminal failure handling after the agent records its terminal intent.
Keep the fix grounded in the existing app-server dynamic tool bridge and runtime completion flow unless a smaller repo-local contract change is insufficient.

tasks.title: Design the terminal turn contract for self-bootstrap lanes
tasks.objective: Decide the smallest runtime-owned contract that makes terminal tracker handoff explicit and prevents saved plan completion from masquerading as run success.
inputs: Current tracker tool bridge, app-server wrapper, and runtime completion rules
outputs: A concrete decision on how the turn must declare terminal tracker completion
An implementation boundary between in-turn signaling and post-run writeback
verification: The chosen contract explains how coding-complete but handoff-missing lanes are rejected
The design leaves the existing review-handoff versus manual-attention model intact

tasks.title: Implement explicit terminal finalization in the runtime path
tasks.objective: Wire the app-server and tracker bridge so the agent must explicitly finalize one terminal tracker path before the run can succeed.
outputs: Code that records or validates an explicit in-turn terminal completion signal
Runtime gating that no longer treats a finished coding turn as sufficient without the terminal signal
verification: The runtime still accepts valid review handoff and manual-attention paths
The XY-156-shaped missing-handoff path fails before Maestro reports a successful run

tasks.title: Align specs and operator guidance with the new terminal contract
tasks.objective: Update the repo-owned docs so the required terminal-finalization rule matches the runtime behavior and pilot expectations.
outputs: Normative text for the explicit terminal turn contract
Operator guidance that explains why plan completion alone is not a successful exit
verification: Spec and guide text use the same terminal-path terminology as the runtime
Docs no longer imply that coding completion alone can end a successful lane

tasks.title: Verify the XY-162 regression fix
tasks.objective: Prove the new contract catches the missing-handoff failure shape and leaves the repo green under the standard checks.
outputs: Focused regression evidence for the missing-handoff path
Passing fmt, lint, and test results
verification: Add or update tests that model the XY-156 terminal-missing path
Run the full defaults.verification_commands set successfully

triggers: The app-server transport cannot express a reliable terminal-finalization contract without a broader protocol change
The fix requires changing external Codex skills instead of repo-local runtime and prompt surfaces
The runtime needs a broader delivery lifecycle redesign beyond the bounded review-handoff versus manual-attention exit model

evidence: 2026-03-18: The retained XY-156 transcript shows the lane ended with implementation and verification complete while commit, push, PR creation, and tracker handoff were still listed as remaining work.

2026-03-18: TrackerToolBridge now exposes `issue_terminal_finalize`, requires a matching explicit terminal-finalization path at turn completion, and rejects both the XY-156 no-signal shape and the review-handoff-without-finalize shape in unit tests.

2026-03-18: The app-server wrapper now calls dynamic-tool completion validation before it marks a run attempt successful, so missing terminal finalization fails inside the run wrapper instead of only later in orchestrator post-processing.

2026-03-18: The child prompt contract now requires `issue_terminal_finalize` after `issue_review_handoff` or manual attention and explicitly forbids ending the turn or marking a saved plan `phase = "done"` before that succeeds.
2026-03-18: docs/spec/system_tracker_tool_contract.md, docs/spec/system_maestro_runtime.md, and docs/guide/pilot.md now describe the explicit terminal-finalization rule consistently.
2026-03-18: Local verification passed for cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, and cargo make test.

context_snapshot.primary_gap: A self-bootstrap lane can end with code and tests complete while terminal tracker handoff remains undone.
